reasoning: The market expectation was around 9% in mid-October. As of late October 2025, Keir Starmer continues to function actively as Prime Minister, engaging in international diplomacy (India trade deal, meetings with Zelenskyy) and outlining domestic plans. Although he has faced significant internal friction, including multiple cabinet/ministerial resignations and calls from the public and political commentators for votes of no confidence, his massive parliamentary mandate and the absence of a formal, active leadership challenge make an imminent exit highly unlikely. Ousting a sitting PM with a large majority is procedurally complex and time-consuming. Furthermore, key rivals touted in the media (such as a prominent non-MP mayor, likely Andy Burnham) are ineligible to immediately challenge for leadership. With only about two months remaining in 2025 and no evidence of sudden ill health or an acute, terminal scandal that would force an immediate resignation, the probability of him ceasing to be PM before December 31, 2025 is very low.
